action: Resolve orchestrator stall at test phase due to 'unknown agent: tester'. The v10.3.0 evolve-tester persona exists at agents/evolve-tester.md but scripts/dispatch/subagent-run.sh's allowed-agent regex (~line 967, parallel-dispatch validator) does NOT include 'tester' — the pattern is '^(intent|scout|tdd-engineer|builder|auditor|inspirer|evaluator|retrospective|orchestrator|plan-reviewer|triage|memo)$'. The single-agent dispatch path likely has a similar gap. Cycle 41 attempt 2 stalled in phase=test for 241s before watchdog SIGTERM, costing a full cycle retry and triggering exit code 144. Fix: (a) add 'tester' to subagent-run.sh agent allowlists (multiple regex sites + .evolve/profiles/tester.json if missing), OR (b) gate the test phase behind an env flag (EVOLVE_TEST_PHASE_ENABLED) until tester is fully wired, so orchestrator's fallback path (legacy audit-only) is the default behavior.
evidence_pointer: https://github.com/mickeyyaya/evolve-loop/commit/2522dbc — salvage commit body documents the cycle 41 attempt 2 stall sequence
operator_note: Cycle 41 attempt 1 fell back gracefully (orchestrator caught 'unknown agent' and used legacy audit). Attempt 2 hung — different code path or transient state issue. Until fixed, every cycle that reaches test phase risks a 4-min watchdog kill. Verified via phase-watchdog log lines 'phase advance: build -> test' then 'WARN: idle for 181s' then 'FIRE: idle for 241s'.
